candidates.risks: Tripled since IPO with ~63x forward PE - severe de-rating risk on any growth deceleration; Blackstone sell-down overhang; roll-up integration risk (growth partly acquired); short public track record through a demand cycle
candidates.whyNotCrowded: Frankly, it increasingly is - which is why it scores a heavy hype penalty and a watchlist zone; the residual alpha is structural (scarce pure-play, forced index buying, under-modeled fabrication margin) rather than discovery-based, and is best harvested on pullbacks like the current ~20% drawdown from the $107 high

candidates.thesis: The razor-blade layer of the AI fiber buildout: world-leading supplier of ultra-precision ceramic/MT ferrules, MPO/MTP multi-fiber connectors, and the polishing machines plus consumables every connector factory and data-center MAC crew needs. AI fabric link counts grow faster than racks (roughly 20-40x more connectors/ferrules and ~20x more polishing consumables per AI DC than legacy), so Seikoh compounds ahead of GPU unit curves. FY3/26 guidance was raised to sales Y30.0B (+25% vs prior) and OP Y7.0B (+43%) after a Q3 OP beat of 44% vs consensus, and the supply-limited input part that capped polisher output has cleared. At ~Y256B (~$1.7B) with zero sell-side coverage, it is still a structurally under-owned bottleneck monopoly.
candidates.latentAsset: Near-monopoly in optical-connector ferrules and connector-endface polishing machines/consumables - a recurring-revenue chokepoint that wins whether links are pluggable (more MPO) or CPO (more external fiber shuffles and patch panels)
candidates.catalysts: FY3/26 full-year results and FY3/27 guidance; MPO densification at 800G/1.6T; CPO-driven fiber-shuffle and patch-panel volume; polisher capacity expansion now that the input bottleneck cleared; consumables mix lifting margins
candidates.risks: Stock already up ~7x in 12 months (PE 41 trailing); Japan small-cap liquidity; customer concentration in connector houses; a sharp pause in hyperscaler fiber-plant construction would hit polisher capex orders first; yen strength
candidates.whyNotCrowded: No sell-side coverage; discovered only by a handful of niche small-cap substacks; absent from every AI ETF/basket; foreign institutional ownership still thin - though the +663% market-cap move means the discovery gap is partially closed, so this is scored honestly with a hype penalty

candidates.thesis: The purest public play on the fiber-to-chip interface for CPO. FOCI is described by DIGITIMES as one of TSMC's closest collaborators on co-packaged optics (COUPE), owning the high-density, high-precision fiber-array-unit (FAU) manufacturing, alignment and packaging technology that every CPO optical engine needs. It is deploying high-precision automated FAU lines now and plans mass production of 1.6T and 3.2T FAUs in 2H26, exactly when NVIDIA's photonics switches and TSMC's first COUPE generation ramp. FAU attach is a per-engine consumable-like content stream that scales with CPO ports, not with transceiver vendor share.
candidates.latentAsset: TSMC-adjacent fiber array unit (FAU) and ReLFACon fiber-to-PIC connector franchise - the mechanical chokepoint of CPO assembly where sub-micron passive alignment know-how is scarce
candidates.catalysts: 1.6T/3.2T FAU mass production in 2H26; TSMC COUPE first-generation ramp; NVIDIA Quantum-X/Spectrum-X photonics switch shipments through 2026-27; automated line qualification lifting gross margin; potential external-laser (ELS) connector content
candidates.risks: Loss-making on TTM basis with ~NT$1.9B revenue against a NT$94B cap (~50x sales) - the CPO ramp is heavily pre-paid; +244% 1-year move with high retail churn; CPO timing slips would hit hard; competition from US Conec/Senko/Browave ecosystem; thin disclosure in English
candidates.whyNotCrowded: Crowded with Taiwan retail but essentially unknown to and un-ownable by most Western institutions (TPEx listing, no ADR, no sell-side coverage outside local brokers); not in any global AI basket; the structural FAU-chokepoint story is still not consensus outside Taiwan - hype penalty applied for the local run

candidates.thesis: Swiss fiber-connectivity industrial whose Data Center growth initiative just inflected: group order intake hit a record CHF 1,032M in 2025 (+13.7%) on major data-center orders in the Communication segment, with EBIT margin up to 10.5% despite FX headwinds. H+S supplies pre-terminated high-fiber-count ribbon cables, MPO assemblies and polarity management for AI clusters (AI racks need roughly 36x the fiber of CPU racks), and is the cabling/connectivity partner co-developing hollow-core fiber deployments with Microsoft Azure - the only merchant industrial with certified HCF field cabling. The 2025 order book converts to 2026-27 revenue while rail/industry segments provide downside ballast.
candidates.latentAsset: Hyperscaler-certified pre-terminated fiber cabling franchise plus the Microsoft Azure hollow-core fiber cabling co-development - a latent optical-infrastructure asset buried inside a diversified Swiss rail/industrial connector company
candidates.catalysts: Conversion of record 2025 order intake into H1 2026 sales (results July/August 2026); Azure hollow-core fiber moving from trials to volume deployment; next-gen higher-density HCF cable designs; further data-center frame agreements
candidates.risks: Trailing PE ~64x against 2025 sales that declined 3.3% (FX/copper/portfolio) - the multiple leans entirely on the order-to-revenue conversion; stock nearly 3x off its 52-week low (CHF 83.30 to 288.00 range); CHF strength; transportation segment cyclicality; data-center orders can be lumpy
candidates.whyNotCrowded: A Swiss mid-cap classified as an industrial, not a tech name - absent from AI/optics baskets and US investor screens; European value funds own it for rail; the data-center fiber and hollow-core angles are barely covered in English-language research, though the 2025 rerating means it is no longer undiscovered

candidates.thesis: The merchant epitaxy layer of the laser-diode chain. VPEC grows InP and GaAs epi wafers (InGaAs PD/APD, laser/EML structures) for optical-communication chipmakers and has entered Furukawa Electric's 800G transmitter supply chain - meaning it sits one layer upstream of every EML/CW-laser shortage. Peer LandMark's own disclosure (SiPh epi output 'far below customer needs', capex doubling for 2H27 demand) confirms merchant III-V epi capacity is a genuine bottleneck; VPEC is the cheaper, more diversified way to own that layer after LandMark's rerating to ~130x sales made it untouchable. Watchlist-grade until InP momentum shows up decisively in monthly revenue.
candidates.latentAsset: Merchant InP/GaAs epitaxial wafer capacity for photodetectors and laser diodes - the upstream input of the EML/CW-laser chain (Furukawa, Lumentum, Coherent ecosystem) where capacity additions take 18+ months
candidates.catalysts: InP PD/APD and EML epi ramp for 800G/1.6T; CW laser epi demand from silicon photonics/CPO external light sources; Furukawa supply-chain share gains; monthly TWSE revenue prints as the real-time tell; capacity expansion announcements
candidates.risks: 125x trailing PE on only ~11% TTM revenue growth - the market is pre-paying for an InP ramp that 2025 numbers (+4.3%) have not yet validated; legacy GaAs RF (handset) exposure dilutes the optics story; LandMark/IQE/internal-epi competition; +262% market-cap move already
candidates.whyNotCrowded: Not in the universe and invisible to Western funds (no ADR, minimal English coverage); the AI flow into Taiwan optics has concentrated in LandMark and the transceiver names, leaving VPEC as the second-look epi play - but local money has clearly found it, hence the hype penalty and watchlist zone

factIssues: Market cap slightly overstated: ~$3.2B (JPY 518.3B at ~160 USD/JPY, Jun 9 2026 Tokyo close of JPY 13,370) vs claimed ~$3.4B; stock fell ~7.7% recently, so the claim is stale rather than wrong.

'Dominant' is mildly overstated: MJC is #3 in the overall probe card market (~14% share) behind FormFactor and Technoprobe; in memory/DRAM probe cards it is a co-leading top-2 player alongside FormFactor (memory ~80% of its probe card sales), with JEM and Korean vendors (Korea Instrument, Will Technology) also competing in HBM test - oligopoly co-leader, not sole-dominant.

Pitch context caveat (not a fact error): the name was a heavily-publicized HBM hype stock in 2024, so 'hidden/under-owned' framing should be independently validated against current ownership/crowding data.

No material factual errors. Nuance: first-mover claim is supported (Cube Prober supplied to Samsung, passed SK hynix evaluation, in qualification at Micron), but it is not sole-source forever — SK hynix is reportedly co-developing HBM4 system-level test equipment with TSMC, and Genesem supplies sorters used in HBM test, so competitive entry into the insertion is emerging.

6871.T.reasons: Survives, but only for a mid-table slot. REALITY: solid - May 2026 guidance raise verified (Q2 sales Y45.7B / OP Y12.9B, 9-mo Y71.6B / Y21.9B, >26% YoY probe card growth, capex Y15.3B->Y19.0B); memory probe cards ARE the business (global #1, ~33% memory probe-card share), so AI/HBM exposure is core revenue, not narrative. REDUNDANCY: not killed - universe holds FormFactor (foundry/logic-weighted) and Technoprobe (logic-centric); Micronics is the purest DRAM/HBM probe-card consumable play, not strictly dominated by either. CROWDING: the real weakness - stock up ~245-295% in 1 year (52wk range Y3,920-17,890), ATH in May 2026, and the pitch's headline catalyst (the guidance raise) already caused a 17% one-day pop, so entry is post-rerating. Mitigants: only 2 covering analysts (PT Y9,900, ~26% below market), ~$3.3B cap, fwd P/E ~24 on 26%+ growth, absent from Western AI baskets - discovered but not consensus-saturated. Pitch's own mid-table self-handicap is appropriate; reject any top-quartile placement, and note memory-cycle downside (prior-quarter order decline shows the consumable line is still cyclical). Sources: investing.com earnings coverage, simplywall.st, stockanalysis.com/quote/tyo/6871, tradingview.com TSE:6871.
